title: Learning Equilibria from Data: Provably Efficient Multi-Agent Imitation Learning

repos.name: tfreihaut__Murmail
repos.evidence: README states explicitly: 'This is the repository of the paper: "Learning equilibria from data: Provably efficient Multi-Agent Imitation Learning"'. Repo owner 'tfreihaut' matches first author Till Freihaut. Contains murmail.py (Algorithm 2 / MURMAIL), behavior_cloning.py (BC baseline), innerloop_rl.py (UCBVI inner loop), game_solver.py (Nash solver), experiment.ipynb (Figure 2 driver), and committed output PNGs Experiment_C_2/1000/10000/inf.png matching paper's four Figure 2 panels.

rationale: The single cloned repo tfreihaut/Murmail is unambiguously the paper's own implementation. The README directly names the paper, the repo owner matches first author Till Freihaut (tfreihaut@github / freihaut@ifi.uzh.ch), and the code implements the paper's two novel algorithms (MURMAIL and BC) with the exact experimental setup described in Section 5 and Appendix J. The audit correctly identified this as the core repo and wrote substantive findings against it (a medium-severity bug in the mirror-descent update, two low-severity differences in hyperparameters/expert generation, and two low-severity missing-code issues). No non-core repos were cloned. All findings are correctly attributed to the actual paper code.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified the core repo and produced well-grounded findings. No misclassification or misdirected findings detected.
recommended_action: No corrective action needed. The audit is correctly scoped to the paper's own implementation. The medium-severity bug (single-sample mirror-descent update in murmail.py:390-397) and the difference finding about tuned hyperparameters are the most actionable items for the authors.
